testCases.title: Admin login grants access to admin-only tabs and controls
testCases.description: Verifies the core auth boundary — a successful admin login must reveal admin-only functionality (Messages, Activity, Requests, admin tools) and hide public-only UI (Contact button, NDIS banner/tab, Install App button), since every other admin-path test case depends on this working.
steps: Load the app as a signed-out visitor.
Confirm Messages/Activity/Requests tabs and the admin tools dropdown are not visible.
Open the Admin login modal and sign in with valid admin credentials.
Observe the tab bar and header controls after login.
testCases.expectedResult: After login, Messages/Activity/Requests tabs and admin tools appear; Contact button, NDIS Guide, and Install App button are hidden.

testCases.title: Public visitor can create a new Professional entry directly
testCases.description: Creating directory entries is the app's primary purpose and is explicitly allowed without login — this must work for an unauthenticated visitor with no request/approval step involved.
steps: As a signed-out visitor, open the Professionals tab.
Click "+ Add professional".
Fill in a required name and select at least one specialty.
Submit the form.
Confirm the new entry appears in the Professionals list immediately.
testCases.expectedResult: The new professional entry is created and visible right away, with no approval step required.

testCases.title: Non-admin editing an existing entry creates a pending request, not a live change
testCases.description: This is the core data-integrity safeguard for the whole app — a non-admin must never be able to directly modify existing data; edits must be redirected into the delete-requests moderation queue.
steps: As a signed-out visitor, open any existing Professional entry and click Edit.
Change a field (e.g. phone number) and submit.
Reload the Professionals list and confirm the original entry is unchanged.
Log in as admin and open the Requests tab.
testCases.expectedResult: The original entry's data is untouched after the non-admin "edit"; the proposed change appears in Requests as a pending edit request with both original and proposed values shown.

testCases.title: Non-admin deleting an existing entry creates a pending removal request requiring a reason
testCases.description: Mirrors the edit-request safeguard for deletions — a non-admin delete action must never remove live data directly, and the removal-request flow must enforce a reason field.
steps: As a signed-out visitor, open any existing entry (any section) and click Delete.
Attempt to submit the removal request with no reason entered.
Enter a reason and submit.
Reload the list and confirm the entry still exists; log in as admin and check Requests.
testCases.expectedResult: Submitting with no reason is blocked; after entering a reason, the entry remains live and a pending delete request (with reason) appears in the admin Requests queue.

testCases.title: Admin approving a pending request applies the change to live data
testCases.description: Completes the moderation loop started by the two cases above — an approved request must actually be applied to the underlying collection, and a denied one must not be.
steps: As admin, open the Requests tab with at least one pending edit request.
Review the shown diff (original vs. proposed values).
Click Approve.
Navigate to the affected entry in its section.
testCases.expectedResult: The entry now reflects the proposed changes, and the request is removed from the pending Requests queue.

testCases.title: Restore-from-backup requires explicit destructive confirmation before replacing data
testCases.description: Restore deletes and recreates the professionals, products, families, and marketplace collections wholesale — the highest blast-radius action in the app. It must never run without an explicit "REPLACE everything" confirmation, and cancelling must leave existing data untouched.
steps: As admin, take a Backup (downloads a JSON export).
Open Restore and select the backup JSON file.
When the destructive confirmation dialog appears, click Cancel.
Confirm existing data is unchanged; repeat Restore and this time confirm the destructive dialog.
testCases.expectedResult: Cancelling leaves all existing data untouched. Confirming replaces professionals/products/families/marketplace with the backup's contents exactly.

testCases.title: Admin login with invalid credentials shows an error and grants no access
testCases.description: The failure path of the login flow must surface a clear error and must not leave the app in a partially-authenticated state.
steps: Open the Admin login modal.
Enter a valid-looking but incorrect email/password combination.
Submit.
testCases.expectedResult: A visible error message appears; admin-only tabs remain hidden and the visitor stays in the signed-out state.

testCases.title: Marketplace listing price is required only for Sell/Rent, not Donate/Lend
testCases.description: Price is conditionally required based on listing type — this cross-field rule is easy to regress and directly affects whether sellers can post valid listings.
steps: Open the Marketplace "+ Add listing" form.
Select type "Donate", leave price blank, fill other required fields, submit.
Repeat with type "Sell" and price left blank — attempt submit.
Enter a price greater than 0 for the "Sell" listing and submit.
testCases.expectedResult: Donate submits successfully with no price. Sell is blocked until a price greater than 0 is entered, then submits successfully.

testCases.title: Marketplace listing requires at least a phone number or an email
testCases.description: Contact info is validated as "at least one of phone or email" rather than both being independently optional — a real user must be reachable by the poster.
steps: Open the Marketplace "+ Add listing" form and fill all required fields except phone and email.
Attempt to submit.
Fill in only an email (leave phone blank) and submit.
testCases.expectedResult: Submission is blocked with neither phone nor email filled; submission succeeds once at least one is provided.

testCases.title: Family form rejects a suburb that wasn't selected from the autocomplete list
testCases.description: Suburb must come from the address-autocomplete selection, not free-typed text, since downstream features (map pins, postcode auto-fill) depend on a resolved suburb value.
steps: Open the Families "+ Add family" form.
Type a suburb name in the Suburb field but do not select a suggestion from the dropdown.
Fill other required fields and attempt to submit.
Retype the suburb and select a suggestion from the autocomplete dropdown, then submit.
testCases.expectedResult: Submission is blocked when the suburb wasn't chosen from a suggestion; it succeeds once a suggestion is explicitly selected, with postcode auto-filled.

testCases.title: Visitor can add a star rating and comment review to a Professional listing
testCases.description: Reviews are a core engagement feature on professional listings and should save and display correctly.
steps: Open an existing Professional entry.
Check "leave a review", select a star rating, and enter a comment.
Submit.
Reopen the entry.
testCases.expectedResult: The review (rating + comment) is saved and visible on the professional's entry.

testCases.title: Contact form submission is visible to admin in the Messages tab
testCases.description: The public Contact modal is the only way a visitor reaches an admin directly outside the moderation-request flow, so it must reliably create a readable message for admin.
steps: As a signed-out visitor, open the Contact modal.
Fill in required name and message fields, and submit.
Log in as admin and open the Messages tab.
testCases.expectedResult: The submitted message appears in the admin Messages inbox with the submitted name and message text.

alreadyCovered: Homepage loads and shows the main heading — covered by the existing Homepage UI > loads and shows the main heading test in e2e-testing/tests/ui/example.spec.js

notIncluded.title: NDIS Guide static content displays correctly and is hidden when admin is logged in
Last active tab persists across a page reload
Install App button behavior differs correctly between iOS/Safari and Android/Chrome, and hides once installed
Card view / table view toggle works correctly per section
Service worker falls back to cached app shell when offline
Admin denying a pending request removes it from the queue without applying the change
Photo upload exceeding the 5MB size cap is rejected on Family/Marketplace forms
Directory search and filter (by specialty/suburb/category) returns correct results
Activity log filters entries correctly by date range

unclearImpact: No firestore.rules or storage.rules files exist in the repo, so server-side access control (e.g. whether an unauthenticated client could write directly to ds-network-activity or ds-network-messages by calling the Firestore API directly) can't be confirmed from code alone.
ds-network-data legacy collection and its one-time migration functions appear to be transitional — unclear whether this migration path is still expected to run in production or is dead code.

P1-4 (family suburb autocomplete) was not automated: the suburb field calls the live Google Places API, which is blocked for localhost in this dev environment, so the 'select a suggestion and submit successfully' step cannot be executed here.
